verdict_reasoning: KLK2 (kallikrein-related peptidase 2) is a secreted serine protease. It is synthesized with an N-terminal signal peptide that directs it to the ER/Golgi secretory pathway. The mature protein is released into the extracellular space (blood, seminal fluid, urine) but does not contain a transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or any other mechanism to anchor itself to the plasma membrane or remain stably associated with the outer leaflet. It functions as a soluble extracellular protease. While it may transiently interact with cell-surface receptors or substrates, it does not reach or remain on the outer face of the PM via its own mechanism. Secreted-only proteins without membrane anchors are excluded from surface accessibility.
